2603.27438.title: The Novelty Bottleneck: A Framework for Understanding Human Effort Scaling in AI-Assisted Work
2603.27438.paper_summary: We propose a stylized model of human-AI collaboration that isolates a mechanism we call the novelty bottleneck: the fraction of a task requiring human judgment creates an irreducible serial component analogous to Amdahl's Law in parallel computing. The model assumes that tasks decompose into atomic decisions, a fraction $ν$ of which are "novel" (not covered by the agent's prior), and that specification, verification, and error correction each scale with task size. From these assumptions, we derive several non-obvious consequences: (1) there is no smooth sublinear regime for human effort it transitions sharply from $O(E)$ to $O(1)$ with no intermediate scaling class; (2) better agents improve the coefficient on human effort but not the exponent; (3) for organizations of n humans with AI agents, optimal team size decreases with agent capability; (4) wall-clock time achieves $O(\sqrt{E})$ through team parallelism but total human effort remains $O(E)$; and (5) the resulting AI safety profile is asymmetric -- AI is bottlenecked on frontier research but unbottlenecked on exploiting existing knowledge. We show these predictions are consistent with empirical observations from AI coding benchmarks, scientific productivity data, and practitioner reports. Our contribution is not a proof that human effort must scale linearly, but a framework that identifies the novelty fraction as the key parameter governing AI-assisted productivity, and derives consequences that clarify -- rather than refute -- prevalent narratives about intelligence explosions and the "country of geniuses in a data center."
2603.27438.paper_summary_zh: 我們提出了一個人類與人工智慧合作的風格化模型，該模型孤立出一個我們稱之為新穎瓶頸的機制：一項任務中需要人類判斷的部分創造了一個不可簡化的串行組件，類似於並行計算中的阿姆達爾法則。該模型假設任務分解為原子決策，其中一部分 $ν$ 是「新穎的」（不在代理的先前知識範疇內），並且規範、驗證和錯誤修正的規模均與任務大小相關。根據這些假設，我們推導出幾個不明顯的結果：（1）人類努力的平滑亞線性範圍不存在，它會從 $O(E)$ 突然轉變為 $O(1)$，沒有中間的縮放類別；（2）更好的代理改善了人類努力的係數，但不改變指數；（3）對於擁有 n 名人類和 AI 代理的組織，最佳團隊規模隨著代理能力的提高而減少；（4）牆鐘時間通過團隊並行性達到 $O(\sqrt{E})$，但總人類努力仍然保持在 $O(E)$；（5）由此產生的 AI 安全特徵是不對稱的——AI 在前沿研究上受到瓶頸限制，但在利用現有知識上則不受限制。我們展示這些預測與 AI 編碼基準、科學生產力數據和從業者報告的實證觀察一致。我們的貢獻不是證明人類努力必須線性增長，而是一個框架，確定新穎比例是主導 AI 輔助生產力的關鍵參數，並推導出澄清——而不是反駁——關於智能爆炸和「數據中心中的天才國度」的普遍敘事的結果。
2603.27438.author: Jacky Liang et.al.
